slides.title: For Erumaaa
slides.subtitle: A presentation in 41 slides. Promise it's worth it.

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Applicant for the position of Husband
slides.experience: Several years of being absolutely, embarrassingly in love with Erumaaa.
slides.oss_experience: References available on request. (My mom thinks I'm a great catch.)

slides.title: How we got here
slides.subtitle: A brief history of the best thing that ever happened to me.

slides.title: The first time I saw you

box.content:
I remember it the way most people remember big news. A small jolt. A sudden, quiet certainty that this was someone I should pay attention to.

I didn't know yet that paying attention to you would become the easiest, most natural thing I'd ever do.

You said something that made me laugh harder than I had in months. And then — even better — you said something thoughtful, the kind of sentence that stays with you for days.

I went home and did the worst thing a person can do at the start of something good: I overthought it. I drafted texts I didn't send. I refreshed my chat at unholy hours.

And then I sent the only one that mattered.

box.title: There wasn't one moment. There were a hundred small ones.

list.text: The way you hold a cup with both hands, like the world's at stake
Your laugh at things you weren't supposed to laugh at
How you actually think before you answer questions
The book on your bedside table that never seems to make it past chapter three
That you don't mind silence — you make it feel like company
That you reply when you mean to, not on a schedule
How you tell me when I'm being a fool — gently, but you tell me

slides.title: Quick performance review
slides.subtitle: Life before Erumaaa. Life after Erumaaa. A scientific comparison.

slides.title: The data is in
leftPoints: 🛌 Slept five hours and called it productivity
🎧 Listened to the same twelve songs on loop
📱 Doomscrolled like it was a paid position
🤐 Kept feelings in a small dusty container
🧦 Owned exactly two pairs of matching socks
rightPoints: 🍳 Suddenly a passable cook (you raised the bar)
🌅 Sleeping properly, because you insist on it
🎶 Stealing your taste in music (yours is better)
🛒 Always buying snacks I know you'll like
✨ Genuinely happier in ways friends keep noticing

note.text: Conclusion: substantial quality-of-life improvements across every measured metric. 12 out of 10. Would recommend to a friend.

slides.title: Why I love you
slides.subtitle: The actual answer. Not the cute one.

box.content:
You ask the questions other people are too tired or too cool to ask.

You change your mind when the evidence changes — which is a kind of bravery most people pretend they don't need. You notice things. Small details. Throwaway lines. And you remember them weeks later, often when I've forgotten I said them.

Being smart is common. Being curious in the way you are — that's rare.

You are kind to people you'll never see again. You're kind to the friend who's been hard work this year. You're kind to me on days I haven't earned it.

Kindness isn't softness. The way you do it, it looks like strength.

It's louder than you mean it to be. It comes out at the wrong moments. It makes strangers turn around in restaurants.

It is, without exaggeration, my favourite sound on this entire planet.

If we have a soundtrack, that's it.

It's not that we never fight. It's that we know how to come back.

It's not that we agree on everything. It's that we genuinely like each other's company even when we don't.

That, I've come to learn, is the whole thing. Everything else is decoration.

slides.title: You asked. I answered.
slides.subtitle: Every question you've actually thrown at me, in convenient multiple-choice form.

slides.question: Why do you want to marry me?
options: Because life is more interesting when you're in the room
Because every plan I make now starts with the word "we"
Because you make me a better me — without ever asking me to be different
All of these. Plus forty-seven other reasons saved for slide 29.
slides.explanation: Trick question. The real answer is the entire rest of this deck. But if you want it in one line: because a life with you is the one I keep choosing — every day, in every weather.

slides.question: What if I drive you crazy someday?
options: You already do. (And it's fine.)
Then I drive you crazy right back, and we call it even
We argue, we apologise, we order biriyani, we move on
All of the above. Repeat as needed for the next sixty years.
slides.explanation: The "driving each other crazy" part isn't a bug — it's part of the deal. Every long marriage I've ever watched has two people who occasionally want to throttle each other — gently — and keep choosing each other anyway.

slides.question: What's the best thing about us?
options: We're better at being us than at being apart
We make each other laugh in places we really shouldn't
We tell each other the truth — even the inconvenient parts
Honestly? All three. That's the whole shortlist.
slides.explanation: Couples are easy when it's easy. The good ones are the ones who keep choosing each other when it isn't. We do that. We've already proven it more than once.

slides.question: What scares me about a life without you?
options: Eating dinner alone every night, badly
Forgetting what real conversation sounds like
Going back to the version of me I was before you
All of them. Which is why we're not finding out.
slides.explanation: I'm not afraid of much in life. I am, very specifically, afraid of a version of it that doesn't have you in it. So I'm doing the rational thing and proposing.

slides.question: What do I notice about you most?
options: The small dance you do when food arrives
The way your eyes go quiet when you're thinking hard
How you defend the things you love, even when no one's attacking them
All three. Plus a hundred more I'm collecting like trading cards.
slides.explanation: I have an entire mental file. I take notes I don't write down. I notice things — especially you.

slides.question: What's my favourite thing we do together?
options: Long walks where we forget where we were going
Cooking something neither of us has made before, badly
Saying absolutely nothing in the same room for hours
Trick question. The activity doesn't matter. You're the part I like.
slides.explanation: Genuinely. I have done all sorts of things with you. The fun part is always you.

slides.question: What do I love about your family?
options: They made you. So immediately — all of that.
The way they argue about food like it's high diplomacy
How loud the love is — no one whispers about how they feel
All of the above. They feel like home before I even step inside.
slides.explanation: You can tell a lot about a person by their family. I'm marrying into a family I'd choose. That's a gift not everyone gets.

slides.question: What's our most ridiculous fight going to be?
options: Whose turn it is to text the parents
The exact correct way to fold towels
Pineapple on pizza (an ongoing diplomatic crisis)
Doesn't matter — we'll forget the original reason within an hour anyway.
slides.explanation: Healthy couples fight about laundry and pizza toppings. We are squarely in the laundry-and-pizza tier. Long may it last.

slides.question: What kind of husband am I planning to be?
options: The one who actually listens (not the one waiting for his turn to talk)
The one who remembers important dates without phone reminders (mostly)
The one you can be your full, unfiltered self around
All three. With version updates released as needed.
slides.explanation: I'm not promising perfect. I'm promising I'll keep getting better. Every release will include performance improvements and minor bug fixes.

slides.question: What do I want our home to feel like?
options: Loud, with the right people in it
Quiet, when the world outside is too loud
Like the safest place either of us has ever been
All three — depending on the day.
slides.explanation: If I had to pick one, it's the third. Home should be the place you exhale. Everything else is decoration.

slides.question: What's the first promise I'm making?
options: To say sorry first when I'm wrong
To say sorry first sometimes even when I'm not — because being right is overrated
To never stop being curious about you
All three. They come as a package.
slides.explanation: Promises are easy to make. Mine are: be honest. Be kind. Keep paying attention. The hard part is the daily work of doing them. I'm in.

slides.question: What's something I'd never tell anyone — but I'll tell you?
options: Most days, I'm just winging it
I get unreasonably proud when I make you laugh
I'm a little scared of the future — and a lot less scared because of you
All of them. The whole vault. No locks for you.
slides.explanation: Marriage is, among other things, the official end of having to perform a version of yourself. I'm ready for that. With you, I'm mostly already there.

slides.question: What's the deal-breaker for me?
options: Cheating
Cruelty
Giving up on each other quietly, without telling each other
All three. They're the same thing wearing different costumes.
slides.explanation: Most things I can work through with you. Those three I can't. I don't think we'll ever come close. But the line deserves to be named — out loud, on the record.

slides.question: What if we get really, really old together?
options: We become those slightly annoying old people who finish each other's sentences
We accidentally adopt too many cats
We become the cool grandparents — with stories the kids are slightly afraid of
All of them. Plus matching cardigans. I'm choosing them now.
slides.explanation: I have thought about this in genuinely embarrassing detail. The cardigans will be navy. Resistance is futile.

slides.question: How sure am I that I want to spend my life with Erumaaa?
options: Pretty sure
Very sure
Embarrassingly, indecently, completely sure
All of the above — but mostly the third one
slides.explanation: I have considered this from every angle, including the angles you've made fun of me for considering. The answer keeps coming back the same.

slides.title: What I want next
slides.subtitle: And by "what I want next", I mean "everything, with you".

box.content:
Sunlight on the kitchen counter. The kettle hissing. You — hair still doing whatever it does in the morning. No phones for the first ten minutes. The world can wait.

I want a thousand of these. I want a few thousand more.

list.text: Somewhere cold, where we have to bundle up and you complain affectionately
Somewhere warm, where the wifi is bad and we don't care
Somewhere old, with stories in the stones
And, of course, every restaurant you've been quietly making a list of
A slow train through a country with too many vowels in its name
Anywhere we can do absolutely nothing together, well

box.content:
I'm not promising sunshine. I'm promising that whatever rain comes — and there will be rain — I'd rather get drenched standing next to you than be dry, alone.

You should know I mean this. I've thought about it on bad days, not only the good ones.

I want to know what you look like with grey hair. I want to know the silly things we'll argue about in twenty years.

I want to be the person you call first, and last, and at every important moment in between.

I want the long version of this story. The whole book. Every chapter.

Not in a young, reckless way. In the way you become sure of certain things after watching them hold up under weight.

We have been weighed. We held.

slides.title: Because waiting is fear in disguise.

box.content:
I could wait for some perfect moment. The world has a way of making you wait yourself out of the things that matter.

I don't want to wait myself out of you.

Marriage isn't the end of a love story. It's a beginning that gets to call itself by a different name.

I want to begin. I want everything that comes after this — to come after this with you.

slides.title: Will you marry me?
slides.subtitle: I love you. I am yours. — A.

slides.title: Whatever you say
slides.message: I love you. Now. Tomorrow. Always. — Aravind
